tables.description: UHDR 3 - Data Describing the Emotional Status

variables.name: FAINOTBD
variables.description: 65. Get in and out of bed

variables.name: ELIGIBIL
variables.description: Is the Subject eligible for enrollment?

variables.name: INEX1
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 1:   Is the subject age 18 to 70 years old?

variables.name: INEX10
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 10:   Has the subject been exposed to any investigational drug within 30 days of the Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX11
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 11:  Has the subject used supplemental creatine at a dose greater than 10 grams daily within 30 days of Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX12
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 12:  Has the subject used supplemental Coenzyme Q10 at a dose higher than 300 milligrams daily within 30 days of Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX13
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 13:  Does the subject have a creatine level greater than 2.0 mg/dL at Screening Visit?

variables.name: INEX15A
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15a:  Has the subject used diuretics, cimetidine or probenecid?

variables.name: INEX15B
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15b:  Does the subject have a history of bipolar disorder?

variables.name: INEX15C
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15c:  Has the subject had seizures within 12 months of screening or who are otherwise at heightened risk for seizures in the judgment of the clinical investigator?

variables.name: INEX15D
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15d:  Does the subject have cardiac arrhythmias requiring medical treatment?

variables.name: INEX15E
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15e:  Does the subject have a current history of thrombotic disorder?

variables.name: INEX16
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 16:  Does the subject have any laboratory abnormalities at Screening Visit that in the judgement of the investigator would jeopardize safe conduct of study?

variables.name: INEX17
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 17:  Does the subject have current or a history of substance (alcohol or drug) abuse within one year of Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX18
variables.description: Prompt for Inclusion Exclusion 18a-18g:  Does the subject have clinical evidence of unstable medical illness in the investigators judgement?

variables.name: INEX19
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 19:  Does the subject have clinical evidence of unstable psychiatric illness defined as psychosis (hallucinations or delusions), untreated major depression or suicide ideation within 90 days of Baseline Visit?

variables.name: INEX19A
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 19a:  Does the subject have a risk for dehydration based on the judgment of the clinical investigator? (Risk factors may include but are not limited to diarrhea, vomiting, and/or excessive caffeine use.)

variables.name: INEX2
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 2:  Does the subject have clinical features of HD and a confi rmatory family history of HD; OR clinical features of HD and a CAG repeat expansion of at least 36?

variables.name: INEX3
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 3:  Is the subject in Stage I or II of illness (TFC score >= 7)?

variables.name: INEX4
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 4:   Is the subject ambulatory and not requiring skilled nursing care at the time of enrollment?

variables.name: INEX4A
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 4a:   Does the subject have screening eGFR levels of greater than 60 ml/min (as measured by MDRD equation)?

variables.name: INEX5
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 5:   If the subject is a woman of childbearing potential (i.e., those not post-menopausal or surgically sterile) is she non-lactating, have a negative pregnancy test, and agree to use double barrier contraception methods 30 days prior to the Baseline Visit and during the study? Double barrier is defined as a combination of TWO METHODS (forms) of the following: 1) condoms (male or female) with or without a spermicidal agent, 2) diaphragm or cervical cap with spermicide, 3) intrauterine device (IUD), and/or 4) hormonal-based contraceptive.

variables.name: INEX7
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 7:   Is the subject capable of providing informed consent and complying with the trial procedures?

variables.name: INEX8
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 8:   Is the subject able to take oral medication?

variables.name: INEX9
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 9:   Does the subject have a history of known sensitivity or intolerability to creatine monohydrate or dextrose?

variables.name: INEXCA14
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 14 Canada:   Does the subject have an ALT or AST or alkaline phosphatase greater than 2 times the upper limit of normal?

variables.name: INEXCA15
variables.description: Inclusion Exclusion question 15 Canada:   Does the subject have a screening bilirubin of greater than 1.5 the upper limit of normal?

variables.name: MHCAM1E
variables.description: Med Hx CAM - 1e Chiropractic or osteopathic manipulation

variables.name: MHCAM3G
variables.description: Med Hx CAM - 3g Deep breathing exercises

variables.name: MHCAM3H
variables.description: Med Hx CAM - 3h Prayer for your own health

tables.description: Care Giver - Time Spent and Stress Level

variables.name: BURDEN1
variables.description: 1. Do you feel that the care recipient asks for more help than he/she needs?

variables.name: BURDEN10
variables.description: 10. Do you feel your health has suffered because of your involvement with the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN11
variables.description: 11. Do you feel that you dont have as much privacy as you would like because of the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN12
variables.description: 12. Do you feel that your social life has suffered because you are caring for the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN13
variables.description: 13. Do you feel uncomfortable about having friends over because of the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN14
variables.description: 14. Do you feel that the care recipient seems to expect you to take care of him/her as if you were the only one he/she could depend upon?

variables.name: BURDEN15
variables.description: 15. Do you feel that you dont have enough money to care for the care recipient in addition to the rest of your expenses?

variables.name: BURDEN16
variables.description: 16. Do you feel that you will be unable to take care of the care recipient much longer?

variables.name: BURDEN17
variables.description: 17. Do you feel you have lost control of your life since the care recipients illness?

variables.name: BURDEN18
variables.description: 18. Do you wish you could just leave the care of the care recipient to someone else?

variables.name: BURDEN19
variables.description: 19. Do you feel uncertain about what to do about the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN2
variables.description: 2. Do you feel that because of the time you spend with the care recipient that you dont have enough time for yourself?

variables.name: BURDEN20
variables.description: 20. Do you feel you should be doing more for the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN21
variables.description: 21. Do you feel you could do a better job in caring for the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN22
variables.description: 22. Overall, how burdened do you feel in caring for the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN3
variables.description: 3. Do you feel stressed between caring for the care recipient and trying to meet other responsibilities for your family or work?

variables.name: BURDEN4
variables.description: 4. Do you feel embarrassed over the care recipients behavior?

variables.name: BURDEN5
variables.description: 5. Do you feel angry when you are around the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN6
variables.description: 6. Do you feel that the care recipient currently affects your relationship with family members or friends in a negative way?

variables.name: BURDEN7
variables.description: 7. Are you afraid what the future holds for the care recipient?

variables.name: BURDEN8
variables.description: 8. Do you feel the care recipient is dependent upon you?

variables.name: BURDEN9
variables.description: 9. Do you feel strained when you are around the care recipient?
